rationale: Person A asks whether Person B has met Martin lately. Person B replies that they have not seen him for ages, which directly indicates “no” to meeting him lately. This is a clear, direct answer.
Person A asks if Person B is doing something right now. Person B answers that they are sending out an application, which confirms they are currently doing something. This directly supports “yes.”
Person A asks whether Person B usually exercises with Lukas. Person B responds that they train with him at the gym, which confirms the usual exercise arrangement. This is a clear “yes.”
Person A asks whether there was a death in his family. Person B says his grandfather passed away recently, which directly confirms a death occurred. This is a clear “yes.”

Person A asks if Paris is very far from here. Person B replies that it is about 1000 Km away, which strongly implies it is indeed far. While the question is qualitative (“very far”), the numeric distance makes the “yes” interpretation very likely.
